meta_title: Roofing in Boca Raton, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Boca Raton, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free estimates. From $87/mo.
h1: Boca Raton's Most Trusted Roofing Replacement & Repair Specialists
intro:
Boca Raton is one of South Florida's most coveted addresses — and for good reason. From the waterfront estates of East Boca and the manicured communities of Broken Sound to the family-friendly streets of Mission Bay and Boca Del Mar, this city represents some of Palm Beach County's most valuable real estate. With an average home value of $650,000, your roof isn't just a structural necessity — it's a critical investment in the long-term integrity and market value of your property. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand that Boca Raton homeowners expect nothing short of premium craftsmanship, and that's exactly what we deliver.

Situated just 2.5 miles from the Atlantic coastline, Boca Raton sits squarely in a 170 mph wind zone, making the choice of roofing system far more consequential here than in most parts of the country. The salt-laden air that rolls in off the ocean, the intense UV exposure of South Florida summers, and the ever-present threat of tropical systems all conspire to wear down roofing materials faster than almost anywhere else in the United States. Neighborhoods like Town Center and West Boca may feel inland, but they are far from immune — when a major hurricane pushes onshore, wind-driven rain and debris travel miles from the coast, and an aging or improperly installed roof can fail catastrophically within hours.

Approximately 45% of homes in Boca Raton were built before 1994, meaning nearly half the housing stock predates the sweeping building code reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew. If your home falls into that category, your existing roof may have been installed under standards that are now considered dangerously inadequate by today's Florida Building Code requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed roofing projects across every corner of Boca Raton — from charming mid-century homes near FAU's campus and Red Reef Park to luxury residences along the Intracoastal. We bring the experience, licensing, and product quality that this community demands.

section_why.h2: Why Boca Raton Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Projects
section_why.body:
Not every roofing company operating in South Florida has the depth of experience or the commitment to quality that a Boca Raton home deserves. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing was built on a foundation of in-house craftsmanship — we do not subcontract our installations. Every project in Boca Raton is completed by our own trained, licensed roofing crews, giving us complete quality control from the first tear-off to the final inspection. With 60+ years of combined experience among our team, we have seen virtually every roofing challenge that Palm Beach County can present, and we know exactly how to address it.

Our track record speaks for itself: 500+ completed homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, and a reputation as 'The Kings of Service' that we have earned through consistent, transparent, and respectful work with our clients. When you call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you are not handed off to a call center — you work directly with roofing professionals who know Boca Raton's neighborhoods, understand the City of Boca Raton Building Division's permitting process, and can guide you through every step of a project with confidence and clarity.

We also recognize that a roofing replacement is one of the most significant investments a homeowner will make. That is why we offer flexible financing options starting from just $87 per month with $0 down options available, making it possible for homeowners across all of Boca Raton's neighborhoods to access the highest-quality hurricane-rated roofing systems without financial strain. Whether you are a long-time resident of Boca Del Mar or a new homeowner near Mizner Park, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings the same level of dedicated service to every project.

Beyond installation quality, our value lies in what happens after the job is done. We handle the permit process with the City of Boca Raton Building Division from start to finish, coordinate wind mitigation inspections, and provide full documentation to support insurance claims and premium reduction requests. When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not just getting a new roof — you're gaining a long-term roofing partner who stands behind their work.

section_history.h2: Boca Raton's Storm Legacy: What Decades of Hurricanes Have Taught Local Homeowners
section_history.body:
Boca Raton has weathered eight significant storm events since 1990, and each one has left its mark on the local housing stock in different ways. The most formative of these was Hurricane Andrew in 1992, a catastrophic Category 5 storm whose devastation exposed catastrophic flaws in roofing construction standards across South Florida. While Andrew's most severe damage was concentrated further south, communities in Palm Beach County were not spared, and the storm became the defining moment that drove the state to overhaul its building codes entirely. Thousands of Boca Raton homes with pre-Andrew roofs were never brought up to current standards.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered a different kind of destruction to Boca Raton. Moving unusually fast across South Florida from west to east, Wilma generated sustained winds that caused widespread roof damage throughout East Boca and the inland communities near Town Center Mall. Unlike slower-moving storms, Wilma gave residents little time to react, and the roofing failures were swift. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which approached as a Cat 4 and triggered mass evacuations across Palm Beach County. Even though Irma's center tracked further west than initially forecast, sustained tropical-force winds battered Boca Raton for hours, stripping shingles, cracking tile, and exposing underlayment on thousands of homes. Hurricane Dorian in 2019 served as a sobering near-miss that reminded residents just how narrow the margin of safety can be.

The cumulative lesson from these storms is consistent: roofing systems in Boca Raton must be engineered for the worst-case scenario, installed with precision, and maintained proactively. A roof that merely survived the last storm is not necessarily ready for the next one. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's hurricane-rated roofing systems are designed to perform under repeated extreme weather events — not just once, but for the full lifespan of the installation.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code & Palm Beach County Roofing Requirements Every Boca Raton Homeowner Should Know
section_code.body:
Boca Raton falls under the jurisdiction of the Florida Building Code (FBC), 8th Edition, which governs all roofing installations in the state. While Boca Raton is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation applied to Miami-Dade and Broward counties south of a specific boundary — Palm Beach County has its own rigorous requirements that mandate hurricane-rated products throughout. This distinction matters because many homeowners mistakenly assume that being outside the HVHZ means less stringent standards apply. In reality, the 170 mph wind zone designation that covers Boca Raton requires roofing systems to be engineered, tested, and installed to meet specific uplift resistance and water infiltration standards that far exceed national building codes.

All roofing permits in Boca Raton must be pulled through the City of Boca Raton Building Division, and every installation is subject to inspection before the permit can be closed. This process includes verification of product approvals, installation methods, and fastening patterns — all of which must align with the Florida Product Approval (FPA) listings for each roofing system used. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every aspect of this permitting process on behalf of our clients. We submit the documentation, coordinate the inspections, and ensure your project is fully closed and compliant before we consider the job complete.

For homeowners with pre-1994 roofs, a full replacement also triggers requirements for secondary water resistance (SWR) — a self-adhering underlayment layer that provides a watertight seal even if the primary roofing material is compromised during a storm. This requirement, along with enhanced deck attachment standards, significantly improves a roof's performance during hurricane conditions and is a standard part of every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roofing installation in Boca Raton. Proper code compliance is not just about passing an inspection — it is the foundation of a roof that will protect your home for decades.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Shingles & West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Systems Built for Boca Raton
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two product lines that we believe represent the best available roofing solutions for Boca Raton's unique climate and aesthetic demands: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. These brands were selected not for their name recognition alone, but for their demonstrated performance under South Florida's extreme conditions, their compliance with Florida Building Code requirements, and their ability to deliver both beauty and protection at the highest level.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles are a standout choice for Boca Raton homeowners who want the classic look of a dimensional shingle roof with the engineering to back it up. The Landmark series features a dual-layered construction that provides enhanced wind resistance and a Class A fire rating. These shingles carry a Florida Product Approval and are rated to perform in high-wind environments, making them an excellent fit for Palm Beach County's 170 mph wind zone requirements. The Landmark Series is also available in a wide range of colors and profiles, giving homeowners in neighborhoods like Mission Bay and Broken Sound the design flexibility to complement the architectural style of their homes while meeting all code requirements. CertainTeed's transferable warranty program adds further value, protecting not just the current owner but future buyers as well.

For Boca Raton's many Mediterranean, Spanish, and contemporary estate-style homes — particularly in communities near the Intracoastal or along the East Boca corridor — West Lake concrete and clay tile systems offer an unmatched combination of elegance and durability. West Lake tile products carry some of the highest wind-uplift ratings in the industry and have a proven track record of performance through multiple South Florida hurricane seasons. Concrete tile offers exceptional longevity with lifespans that can exceed 50 years, while clay tile provides a timeless aesthetic with equally impressive structural performance. Both options are available in profiles and colorways designed to complement the upscale architecture that defines much of Boca Raton's residential landscape.

section_cost.h2: Roofing Investment & Financing Options for Boca Raton Homeowners
section_cost.body:
In a market where the average home value reaches $650,000, a quality roofing system is one of the highest-return investments a Boca Raton homeowner can make. Industry data consistently shows that a new roof — particularly one installed with premium, hurricane-rated materials — delivers an impressive return at resale, with many South Florida real estate agents noting that buyers in competitive markets like Boca Raton will pay a meaningful premium for a home with a newer, code-compliant roof. Beyond resale value, a properly installed roof reduces the risk of interior water damage, mold remediation costs, and emergency repair expenses that can run into tens of thousands of dollars after a significant storm.

The cost of a full roof replacement in Boca Raton varies depending on roof size, pitch, material selection, and the complexity of the existing system being removed. CertainTeed Landmark architectural shingle systems generally represent a more accessible price point, while West Lake concrete and clay tile installations — given the added structural considerations and material costs — sit at a higher investment level. In either case, the quality of materials and installation methodology matter far more than the initial price tag. A low-cost installation using inferior materials or improper fastening patterns will cost far more in the long run through premature failure, insurance complications, and potential code violations.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes premium roofing accessible through flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. This means you don't have to delay a critical roof replacement because of upfront cost concerns. Our financing programs are designed to fit a range of budgets, allowing homeowners across Boca Raton's diverse neighborhoods — from first-time buyers near the FAU campus to established families in Broken Sound — to access the same level of hurricane-rated protection. We also help clients document roofing improvements for insurance purposes, which can result in annual premium savings that help offset the cost of the installation over time.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums in Boca Raton
section_insurance.body:
Florida's homeowner's insurance market is one of the most challenging in the nation, and Palm Beach County residents — particularly those in coastal cities like Boca Raton — often face some of the highest premium rates in the state. However, one of the most effective tools available to Boca Raton homeowners for reducing insurance costs is a roof replacement using hurricane-rated materials and an installation that qualifies for a wind mitigation credit. Insurance carriers operating in Florida are required to offer premium reductions for homes that demonstrate specific wind-resistance characteristics, and a new code-compliant roof is typically the single largest driver of those credits.

A wind mitigation inspection, conducted by a licensed inspector after your new roof is installed, evaluates factors including roof shape, deck attachment, secondary water resistance, and the wind resistance rating of the roofing product itself. Homes with hip roofs — which are common throughout Boca Raton's residential communities — already have a structural advantage, and pairing that geometry with a high-rated CertainTeed or West Lake roofing system can produce substantial wind mitigation credits. Depending on your carrier and the specific results of your inspection, annual premium reductions of 20% to 40% are not uncommon for Boca Raton homeowners who upgrade to a modern hurricane-rated roofing system. Over the course of a standard insurance policy period, these savings can represent thousands of dollars.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing works with homeowners throughout the post-installation process to ensure they have the proper documentation to submit to their insurance carriers. We provide all product approval numbers, installation records, and warranty documentation needed to support a wind mitigation filing. Many of our clients in Boca Raton have found that the annual insurance savings achieved after their new roof installation significantly offset their monthly financing payment — making the decision to invest in a quality roof not just a safety choice, but a financially sound one.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Boca Raton home needs a full roof replacement versus a repair?
faqs.a: If your roof is more than 20 years old, has sustained visible storm damage such as missing shingles or cracked tile, shows evidence of water intrusion in your attic or ceilings, or was installed before Boca Raton adopted post-Andrew building codes, a full replacement is likely the most cost-effective and code-compliant solution. We offer free inspections to assess your specific situation and provide an honest recommendation — repair when appropriate, replacement when necessary.

faqs.q: What does a roof replacement typically cost in Boca Raton, and what financing options are available?
faqs.a: Roofing costs in Boca Raton vary based on home size, pitch, material type, and project complexity. CertainTeed architectural shingle systems are typically more affordable than tile installations, which involve additional structural and labor considerations. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners, making it possible to proceed with a critical replacement without depleting savings. We provide transparent, itemized estimates with no hidden fees.

faqs.q: Does Boca Raton require a permit for roof replacement, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that process?
faqs.a: Yes, all roof replacements in Boca Raton require a permit from the City of Boca Raton Building Division. This includes submission of product approvals, installation plans, and scheduling of city inspections. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application through final inspection closure. We ensure every project is fully compliant with the Florida Building Code and that all required documentation is on file before the job is considered complete.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Boca Raton from start to finish?
faqs.a: Most residential roofing replacements in Boca Raton are completed within one to three days, depending on the size of the home and the material being installed. Tile roofing projects may take slightly longer due to the complexity of the installation. The overall project timeline — including permitting, scheduling, installation, and final inspection — typically spans two to four weeks. We keep our clients updated throughout the process and work to minimize disruption to their daily routine.

faqs.q: Will a new hurricane-rated roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Boca Raton?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. A new roof installed with hurricane-rated materials and proper code-compliant fastening patterns qualifies your home for a wind mitigation inspection, which can result in meaningful insurance premium reductions. Boca Raton homeowners with qualifying roof systems have seen annual savings ranging from 20% to 40% depending on their carrier and coverage level. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all necessary documentation to support your wind mitigation filing, and many clients find that their insurance savings offset a significant portion of their monthly financing costs.

cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free roofing inspection and estimate — our team is ready to bring The Kings of Service experience to your Boca Raton home, with flexible financing starting at $87/mo and zero-down options available.
